text_original: Motse said: At present, in governing ike states the rulers all desire to have their countries wealthy, their population large, and their administration orderly.
text_normalized: Motse said: At present, in governing ike states the rulers all desire to have their countries wealthy, their population large, and their administration orderly.

text_original: But instead of wealth they obtain poverty, instead of an increase they obtain a decrease in population, instead of order they obtain chaos, i.e.
text_normalized: But instead of wealth they obtain poverty, instead of an increase they obtain a decrease in population, instead of order they obtain chaos, i.e.

text_original: they lose what they like but obtain what they dislike.
text_normalized: they lose what they like but obtain what they dislike.

text_original: What is the reason for this?
text_normalized: What is the reason for this?

text_original: Motse said; It is due to the large number of fatalists among the people.
text_normalized: Motse said; It is due to the large number of fatalists among the people.

text_original: The fatalists say: When fate decrees that a man shall be wealthy he will be wealthy; when it decrees poverty, he will be poor; when it decrees a large population, this will be large; and when it decrees a small population this will be small; if order is decreed, there will be order; if chaos, there will be chaos.
text_normalized: The fatalists say: When fate decrees that a man shall be wealthy he will be wealthy; when it decrees poverty, he will be poor; when it decrees a large population, this will be large; and when it decrees a small population this will be small; if order is decreed, there will be order; if chaos, there will be chaos.

text_original: If fate decrees old age, there will be old age; if untimely death, there will be untimely death.
text_normalized: If fate decrees old age, there will be old age; if untimely death, there will be untimely death.

text_original: Even if a man sets himself against his fate, what is the use?
text_normalized: Even if a man sets himself against his fate, what is the use?

text_original: " With this doctrine the rulers are urged above and the people are kept away from their work below.
text_normalized: " With this doctrine the rulers are urged above and the people are kept away from their work below.

text_original: Hence the fatalists are umnagnanimous.
text_normalized: Hence the fatalists are umnagnanimous.

text_original: And their doctrines must be clearly examined.
text_normalized: And their doctrines must be clearly examined.

text_original: Now, how is this doctrine to be examined 1 Motse said: Some standard of judgment must be established.
text_normalized: Now, how is this doctrine to be examined 1 Motse said: Some standard of judgment must be established.

text_original: To expound a doctrine without regard to the standard is similar to determining the directions of sunrise and sunset on a revolving potter’s wheel By this means the distinction of right and wrong, benefit and harm, cannot be known.
text_normalized: To expound a doctrine without regard to the standard is similar to determining the directions of sunrise and sunset on a revolving potter’s wheel By this means the distinction of right and wrong, benefit and harm, cannot be known.

text_original: Therefore there must be three tests.
text_normalized: Therefore there must be three tests.

text_original: What are the three tests?
text_normalized: What are the three tests?

text_original: Motse said: Its basis, its verifiability, and its applicability.
text_normalized: Motse said: Its basis, its verifiability, and its applicability.

text_original: How is it to be based?
text_normalized: How is it to be based?

text_original: It should be based on the deeds of the ancient sage-kings.
text_normalized: It should be based on the deeds of the ancient sage-kings.

text_original: How is it to be verified?
text_normalized: How is it to be verified?

text_original: It is to be verified by the senses of hearing and sight of the common people.
text_normalized: It is to be verified by the senses of hearing and sight of the common people.

text_original: How is it to be applied?
text_normalized: How is it to be applied?

text_original: It is to be applied by adopting it in government and observing its benefits to the country and the people.
text_normalized: It is to be applied by adopting it in government and observing its benefits to the country and the people.

text_original: This is what is meant by the three tests of every doctrine.
text_normalized: This is what is meant by the three tests of every doctrine.

text_original: Some of the gentlemen of the world assume there to be fate.
text_normalized: Some of the gentlemen of the world assume there to be fate.

text_original: Now let us examine the deeds of the sage-kings.
text_normalized: Now let us examine the deeds of the sage-kings.

text_original: In ancient times, the confusion produced by Chieh was replaced by an orderly government by T’ang, the chaos of Chow was turned into order by King Wu.
text_normalized: In ancient times, the confusion produced by Chieh was replaced by an orderly government by T’ang, the chaos of Chow was turned into order by King Wu.

text_original: The times did not alter and the people did not change, yet xmder Chieh and Chow the world was chaotic and under T’ang and Wu it was orderly.
text_normalized: The times did not alter and the people did not change, yet xmder Chieh and Chow the world was chaotic and under T’ang and Wu it was orderly.

text_original: Can it be said that there is fate?
text_normalized: Can it be said that there is fate?

text_original: But the gentlemen of the world still assume that there is fate.
text_normalized: But the gentlemen of the world still assume that there is fate.

text_original: Now let us look at some of the writings of the early kings.
text_normalized: Now let us look at some of the writings of the early kings.

text_original: The writings of the early kings that were issued to the whole country and distributed among the people were the laws Did any of the laws of the early kings ever say: Blessing cannot be invoked and disaster cannot be avoided; reverence will not do any good and cruelty will not do any harm "?
text_normalized: The writings of the early kings that were issued to the whole country and distributed among the people were the laws Did any of the laws of the early kings ever say: Blessing cannot be invoked and disaster cannot be avoided; reverence will not do any good and cruelty will not do any harm "?

text_original: The standards according to which lawsuits were tried and punishments were meted out were the codes of punishment flij.
text_normalized: The standards according to which lawsuits were tried and punishments were meted out were the codes of punishment flij.

text_original: Did any of the codes of punishment of the early kings say: "Blessing cannot be invoked and disaster cannot be avoided; reverence will not do any good and cruelty will not do any harm "?
text_normalized: Did any of the codes of punishment of the early kings say: "Blessing cannot be invoked and disaster cannot be avoided; reverence will not do any good and cruelty will not do any harm "?

text_original: The inspiration by wbicb the armies were organized and the soldiers were commanded to advance or to retreat came from the declarations Did any of the declarations of the early kings say; Blessing cannot be invoked and disaster cannot be avoided; reverence will do no good and cruelty will do no harm?
text_normalized: The inspiration by wbicb the armies were organized and the soldiers were commanded to advance or to retreat came from the declarations Did any of the declarations of the early kings say; Blessing cannot be invoked and disaster cannot be avoided; reverence will do no good and cruelty will do no harm?

text_original: Motse said: I have not enumerated the good books of the empire completely.
text_normalized: Motse said: I have not enumerated the good books of the empire completely.

text_original: As they cannot be exhaustively enumerated, I bmit myself to the most prominent ones, namely, the three above mentioned.
text_normalized: As they cannot be exhaustively enumerated, I bmit myself to the most prominent ones, namely, the three above mentioned.

text_original: And try as we may, we cannot find any bebef in the doctrine of fatalism.
text_normalized: And try as we may, we cannot find any bebef in the doctrine of fatalism.

text_original: Should it not then be abandoned?
text_normalized: Should it not then be abandoned?

text_original: To adopt the fatalists’ doctrine is to overthrow righteousness in the world.
text_normalized: To adopt the fatalists’ doctrine is to overthrow righteousness in the world.

text_original: To overthrow righteousness in the world will establish fate, which is a temptation to the people.
text_normalized: To overthrow righteousness in the world will establish fate, which is a temptation to the people.

text_original: And to offer people temptation is to destroy the people.
text_normalized: And to offer people temptation is to destroy the people.

text_original: Now, why is it that we desire righteousness to be with the superiors?
text_normalized: Now, why is it that we desire righteousness to be with the superiors?

text_original: Because when the righteous are in authority, the world will have order, God, bills and rivers, and the spirits will have their chief sacrificer, and the people will be visited by the great blessings therefrom.
text_normalized: Because when the righteous are in authority, the world will have order, God, bills and rivers, and the spirits will have their chief sacrificer, and the people will be visited by the great blessings therefrom.

text_original: How do we know?
text_normalized: How do we know?

text_original: Motse said: In ancient times, T’ang was given a fief at Po.
text_normalized: Motse said: In ancient times, T’ang was given a fief at Po.

text_original: Making allowance for the irregular boundary lines, his land amounted to about a hundred li square.
text_normalized: Making allowance for the irregular boundary lines, his land amounted to about a hundred li square.

text_original: He worked with the people for mutual love and reciprocal benefit, and shared with them what was in abundance.
text_normalized: He worked with the people for mutual love and reciprocal benefit, and shared with them what was in abundance.

text_original: And he led his people to reverence Heaven and worship the spirits.
text_normalized: And he led his people to reverence Heaven and worship the spirits.

text_original: Thereupon, Heaven and the spirits enriched him, the feudal lords befriended him, the people loved him, and the virtuous came to him.
text_normalized: Thereupon, Heaven and the spirits enriched him, the feudal lords befriended him, the people loved him, and the virtuous came to him.

text_original: Within a single generation he ruled over the empire and headed the feudal lords.
text_normalized: Within a single generation he ruled over the empire and headed the feudal lords.

text_original: Again in ancient times, Kiug Wen was assigned to the state of Ch’i Ghou.
text_normalized: Again in ancient times, Kiug Wen was assigned to the state of Ch’i Ghou.

text_original: Making allowance for the irregular boundary lines, his land amoimted to about a hundred li square.
text_normalized: Making allowance for the irregular boundary lines, his land amoimted to about a hundred li square.

text_original: He worked with his people for mutual love and reciprocal benefit.
text_normalized: He worked with his people for mutual love and reciprocal benefit.

text_original: So those near him enjoyed his government and those distant submitted themselves to his virtues.
text_normalized: So those near him enjoyed his government and those distant submitted themselves to his virtues.

text_original: All who heard of King Wen rose up and rushed over to him.
text_normalized: All who heard of King Wen rose up and rushed over to him.

text_original: The stupid and insolent and those weak in limbs remained where they were and complained: Why not let the land of King Wen extend to this place.
text_normalized: The stupid and insolent and those weak in limbs remained where they were and complained: Why not let the land of King Wen extend to this place.

text_original: Wouldn’t I then also be a subject of King Wen?
text_normalized: Wouldn’t I then also be a subject of King Wen?

text_original: " Thereupon Heaven and the: spirits enriched him, the feudal lords befriended him, the j people loved him and the virtuous came to him.
text_normalized: " Thereupon Heaven and the: spirits enriched him, the feudal lords befriended him, the j people loved him and the virtuous came to him.

text_original: Within 5 a single generation he ruled over the whole empire and headed | the feudal lords.
text_normalized: Within 5 a single generation he ruled over the whole empire and headed | the feudal lords.

text_original: As we have said: When the righteous, are in authority the world will have order, God, hills and rivers, i and the spirits will have their chief sacrificer, and the people will be visited by the great benefits therefrom.
text_normalized: As we have said: When the righteous, are in authority the world will have order, God, hills and rivers, i and the spirits will have their chief sacrificer, and the people will be visited by the great benefits therefrom.

text_original: is how we know it to be so.
text_normalized: is how we know it to be so.

text_original: ' The ancient sage-kings published laws and issued orders to be standards of reward and punishment, and to encourage the virtuous and to obstruct the evil.
text_normalized: ' The ancient sage-kings published laws and issued orders to be standards of reward and punishment, and to encourage the virtuous and to obstruct the evil.

text_original: And so the people were filial to their parents at home and respectful to the elders in the village or the district.
text_normalized: And so the people were filial to their parents at home and respectful to the elders in the village or the district.

text_original: They observed propriety in conduct, moderation in going out and coming in, and decency between men and women.
text_normalized: They observed propriety in conduct, moderation in going out and coming in, and decency between men and women.

text_original: And when they were made to look after the court they would not steal, when they were made to defend a city they would not raise an insurrection.
text_normalized: And when they were made to look after the court they would not steal, when they were made to defend a city they would not raise an insurrection.

text_original: When the lord met with death they would commit suicide, and when the lord was banished they would follow him.
text_normalized: When the lord met with death they would commit suicide, and when the lord was banished they would follow him.

text_original: This is what the superior will reward and what the people will applaud.
text_normalized: This is what the superior will reward and what the people will applaud.

text_original: Now, the fatalists say: Whoever is rewarded by the superior is destined to be rewarded.
text_normalized: Now, the fatalists say: Whoever is rewarded by the superior is destined to be rewarded.

text_original: It is not because of his virtue that he is rewarded." Under these conditions the people would not be filial to their parents at home, and respectful to the elders in the village or the district.
text_normalized: It is not because of his virtue that he is rewarded." Under these conditions the people would not be filial to their parents at home, and respectful to the elders in the village or the district.

text_original: They would not observe propriety in conduct, moderation in going out and coming in, or decency between men and women.
text_normalized: They would not observe propriety in conduct, moderation in going out and coming in, or decency between men and women.

text_original: And, if they were made to look after the court they would steal, if they were made to defend a city they would raise an insurrection.
text_normalized: And, if they were made to look after the court they would steal, if they were made to defend a city they would raise an insurrection.

text_original: If the lord met with death they would not commit suicide, and if the lord were banished they would not accompany him.
text_normalized: If the lord met with death they would not commit suicide, and if the lord were banished they would not accompany him.

text_original: This is what the superior will punish, and what the people will condemn.
text_normalized: This is what the superior will punish, and what the people will condemn.

text_original: The fatalists say: ‘‘Whoever is punished by the superior is destined to be punished.
text_normalized: The fatalists say: ‘‘Whoever is punished by the superior is destined to be punished.

text_original: It is not because of his vice that he is punished." Believing in this, the ruler would not be righteous, the minister would not be loyal, the father would not be affectionate, the son would not be filial, the elder brother would not be brotherly, and the younger brother would not be respectful.
text_normalized: It is not because of his vice that he is punished." Believing in this, the ruler would not be righteous, the minister would not be loyal, the father would not be affectionate, the son would not be filial, the elder brother would not be brotherly, and the younger brother would not be respectful.

text_original: The unnatural adherence to this doctrine is responsible for pernicious ideas and is the way of the wicked.
text_normalized: The unnatural adherence to this doctrine is responsible for pernicious ideas and is the way of the wicked.

text_original: Now how do we know fatalism is the way of the wicked?
text_normalized: Now how do we know fatalism is the way of the wicked?

text_original: In ancient times, the miserable people indulged in drinking and eating and were lazy in their work.
text_normalized: In ancient times, the miserable people indulged in drinking and eating and were lazy in their work.

text_original: Thereupon their food and clothing became insufficient, and the danger of hunger and cold was approaching.
text_normalized: Thereupon their food and clothing became insufficient, and the danger of hunger and cold was approaching.

text_original: They did not acknowledge: I was stupid and insolent and was not diligent at work.’" But they would say: " It is but my lot to be poor." The ancient wicked kings did not control the sensuality of their ears and eyes and the passions of their mind.
text_normalized: They did not acknowledge: I was stupid and insolent and was not diligent at work.’" But they would say: " It is but my lot to be poor." The ancient wicked kings did not control the sensuality of their ears and eyes and the passions of their mind.

text_original: They did not follow their ancestors and so they lost their country and ruined their state.
text_normalized: They did not follow their ancestors and so they lost their country and ruined their state.

text_original: They did not know that they should confess: I am stupid and insolent and was not diligent in attending to government." But they would say: It is but my fate to lose it." The ‘‘ Announcement of Chung Hui " says: " I have heard that the man of Hsia issued orders, pretending them to be fate of Heaven.
text_normalized: They did not know that they should confess: I am stupid and insolent and was not diligent in attending to government." But they would say: It is but my fate to lose it." The ‘‘ Announcement of Chung Hui " says: " I have heard that the man of Hsia issued orders, pretending them to be fate of Heaven.

text_original: God was displeased and destroyed his forces." This tells how T’ang showed Chieh’s belief in fate to be wrong, ‘‘ The Great Declaration " says; ‘‘ Chow became insolent and would not worship God and pushed away the ancestors and spirits without offering them sacrifices.
text_normalized: God was displeased and destroyed his forces." This tells how T’ang showed Chieh’s belief in fate to be wrong, ‘‘ The Great Declaration " says; ‘‘ Chow became insolent and would not worship God and pushed away the ancestors and spirits without offering them sacrifices.

text_original: And he said: ‘ Fortune is with my people,’ and neglected and betrayed his duty.
text_normalized: And he said: ‘ Fortune is with my people,’ and neglected and betrayed his duty.

text_original: Heaven thereupon deserted him and withdrew its protection." This tells how King Wu showed Chow’s belief in fate to be wrong.
text_normalized: Heaven thereupon deserted him and withdrew its protection." This tells how King Wu showed Chow’s belief in fate to be wrong.

text_original: If the doctrine of the fatalist were put to practice, the superiors would not attend to government and the sub-ordinates would not attend to work.
text_normalized: If the doctrine of the fatalist were put to practice, the superiors would not attend to government and the sub-ordinates would not attend to work.

text_original: If the superior does not attend to government, jurisdiction and administration will be in chaos.
text_normalized: If the superior does not attend to government, jurisdiction and administration will be in chaos.

text_original: If the subordinates do not attend to work, wealth will not be sufficient.
text_normalized: If the subordinates do not attend to work, wealth will not be sufficient.

text_original: Then, there will not be wherewith to provide for the cakes and wine to worship and do sacrifice to God, ghosts and spirits above, and there will not be wherewith to tranquillize the multitudes of the world below; there will not be wherewith to entertain the noble guests from without, and there will not be wherewith to feed the hungry, clothe the cold, and care for the aged and weak within.
text_normalized: Then, there will not be wherewith to provide for the cakes and wine to worship and do sacrifice to God, ghosts and spirits above, and there will not be wherewith to tranquillize the multitudes of the world below; there will not be wherewith to entertain the noble guests from without, and there will not be wherewith to feed the hungry, clothe the cold, and care for the aged and weak within.

text_original: Therefore fatalism is not helpful to Heaven above, nor to the spirits in the middle sphere, nor to man below.
text_normalized: Therefore fatalism is not helpful to Heaven above, nor to the spirits in the middle sphere, nor to man below.

text_original: The eccentric belief in this doctrine is responsible for per-nicious ideas and is the way of the wicked.
text_normalized: The eccentric belief in this doctrine is responsible for per-nicious ideas and is the way of the wicked.

text_original: Therefore Motse said: If the gentlemen in the world really desire to have the world rich and do not want to have it poor, desire to have it orderly and dislike to have it in con-fusion, the doctrine of fatalism must be rejected.
text_normalized: Therefore Motse said: If the gentlemen in the world really desire to have the world rich and do not want to have it poor, desire to have it orderly and dislike to have it in con-fusion, the doctrine of fatalism must be rejected.

text_original: It is a great calamity to the world.
text_normalized: It is a great calamity to the world.
